observations: Lines 5-9: opening cadence stays inside the 7-14-word center while using short pressure beats ('Lanes for papers, not for sacks.' / 'That certainty was the danger.') for earned emphasis.

Lines 37-39: Sylvanas terrain-read uses clipped observation chains, then releases into one longer consequence sentence about Andorhal's real defenses; this matches the action/introspection handoff rule.

Lines 119-131: Havel's suspect rundown deliberately stacks fragments ('The ones that stole. The ones that fought.') to mimic field sorting; the pattern stays controlled instead of turning into fragment spam.
Lines 145-145: the alley setup had a participial tail ('watching opposite directions') that softened the snap of the tactical pivot; revised to a cleaner spatial image.
Lines 183-185: the patrol summary now lands on the one-line paragraph 'Enough to carry back to Jaina.' and earns the emphasis by following a longer accumulation sentence.
Lines 251-261: the confrontation beat still uses short declaratives, but the post-fix phrasing keeps the argument from tipping into repeated abstract fragment ladders.

fixes.before: Jaina turned her head once, reading the district with her own different attention.
fixes.after: Jaina turned her head once and read the district with her own different attention.

fixes.before: Corin moved with her, laying the sheets out in the order his mind preferred.
fixes.after: Corin moved with her and laid the sheets out in the order his mind preferred.

fixes.before: Cyndia and Lyriel waited with the horses in a strip of shade, watching opposite directions.
fixes.after: Cyndia and Lyriel waited with the horses in a strip of shade, each turned a different way.

fixes.before: "Those rooms on the west repack line," he said, tapping one square.
fixes.after: "Those rooms on the west repack line," he said and tapped one square.

observations: Line 19: the gate-paper lie had one unnecessary comparative scaffold; revised so Jaina's forged pretext sounds routine instead of narrated as a rhetorical construction.
Lines 71-89: Corin's speech stays plain enough for a frightened junior mage—'smooths the books flat,' 'rats could explain it'—and avoids laboratory or memo diction.

Lines 143-145: the split rationale is tactically concrete, but the prose risked summary-register drift where Sylvanas compares the two investigative halves; that section was checked against the document-voice firewall.
Line 183: 'working assets / two layers' read too much like a field brief; revised into Sylvanas-native wording grounded in bodies on the floor and men above them.
Lines 227-241: 'embedded / tiers / operative / opened' carried briefing language; revised to 'inside,' 'handlers above them,' 'live one,' and 'cut into it.'
Line 271: 'use at speed' was the last abstract process phrase in the closing setup; revised to 'use fast' to keep the thought lived instead of managed.

fixes.before: She had pared the lie down on the ride in until it sounded exactly like the kind of thing a busy office would rather pass along than examine.
fixes.after: She had pared the lie down on the ride in until it sounded routine and therefore beneath curiosity.

fixes.before: By dusk she had the bones of the cell: at least eight working assets, two layers, supplies moving off-ledger through spoilage rooms and repack tables, quiet discipline in the men who skipped the taverns because drunk laborers talked and these men could not afford to.
fixes.after: By dusk she had the cell's bones: at least eight of them, men on the floor and men above them, supplies moving off-ledger through spoilage rooms and repack tables, quiet discipline in the men who skipped the taverns because drunk laborers talked and these men could not afford to.

fixes.before: "At least eight embedded in the district," Sylvanas said. "Two tiers. Clerks and labor below, handlers above."
fixes.after: "At least eight inside the district," Sylvanas said. "Clerks and labor on the floor. Handlers above them."

fixes.before: "If we pull a living operative out tonight, we may get the next tier before the district understands it's been opened."
fixes.after: "If we pull a live one out tonight, we may reach the hands above him before the district knows we've cut into it."

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline — prefer lived phrasing over process phrasing
fixes.before: Jaina had taken the next room to turn her sample notes into something she could use at speed if the night went wrong.
fixes.after: Jaina had taken the next room to turn her sample notes into something she could use fast if the night went wrong.

observations: Line 51: 'trying to hold back a flood with forms' is a fresh image sourced from bureaucracy under strain; it clarifies Corin in one stroke and stays in-bounds.
Line 117: 'the district showed its bones' domesticates the setting through structural read rather than purple spectacle; the image fits Sylvanas's tactical eye.
Line 127: Havel's 'stand like drilled soldiers' is a useful status comparison grounded in soldiery, which the style guide explicitly permits.
Line 169: 'The granary district gave up its people more readily than its rooms' is mild personification, but it serves investigative pressure rather than ornament.
Line 205: 'too near thunder' keeps Corin's excitement vivid without sliding into mixed domains or self-conscious lyricism.
Chapter-wide count after fixes lands at roughly 2.37 fresh images per thousand words, which sits inside the style guide's 2-4 target while keeping most paragraphs image-free.

fixes.before: "You've been watching them like suspects," Sylvanas said.
fixes.after: "You've been watching them as suspects," Sylvanas said.

fixes.before: Too many laborers moving like men who expected knives.
fixes.after: Too many laborers already expecting knives.

observations: Lines 35-37: the first long market paragraph runs seven sentences but stays under the word cap and is immediately followed by a shorter tactical paragraph, so the density is earned.
Lines 119-119: Havel's evidence paragraph is the chapter's only paragraph to brush the 120-word ceiling; it remains acceptable because it actively escalates pattern, suspicion, and consequence.
Lines 143-145: the split decision moves from summary into immediate motion without a clock-time bridge; the alley paragraph begins on consequence, not 'later' scaffolding.
Lines 183-185: the one-line paragraph after the patrol summary gives the middle third the required visual break and resets the page before the assay room.
Lines 233-241: the trestle-table synthesis paragraph bridges rooms by consequence ('They laid it together...') rather than using planner language about transitions.
Lines 269-273: the dense capture-plan paragraph is followed by a single-line interior beat and then the final close, so scene balance returns to motion and interior focus quickly enough.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.sceneBalance — dense setup should pivot cleanly into action
fixes.before: Havel was already sketching shift blocks on the back of a spoiled manifest. Cyndia and Lyriel waited with the horses in a strip of shade, watching opposite directions.
fixes.after: Havel already had shift blocks sketched on the back of a spoiled manifest. Cyndia and Lyriel waited with the horses in a strip of shade, each turned a different way.

fixes.rule: paragraphLengthGuidelines.sceneBalance — keep pressure concrete inside the confrontation beat
fixes.before: Havel looked between them, jaw tight, needing someone to choose before the district chose for them.
fixes.after: Havel looked between them, jaw tight. He needed someone to choose before the district chose for them.

observations: Chapter-wide attribution mix is compliant: 42 plain said/asked tags, 1 stronger tag ('echoed'), and the rest carried by clean action beats or two-speaker clarity.
Lines 11-31: the gate exchange uses no-frills tags and one sharp untagged line ('Orders?'), which fits the scene's bureaucratic impatience.
Lines 57-95: Corin's introduction stays readable despite rapid interruptions because tags and speaker order stay clean; no creative-tag clutter survived.
Lines 111-139: Havel's office scene relies on said/asked plus physical beats ('jaw set,' 'turned to the window') instead of ornamental speech verbs.
Lines 193-225: the assay-room exchange keeps Jaina's explanation lucid by alternating short Sylvanas prompts with longer Jaina answer blocks; clarity never depends on fancy attribution.
Lines 241-267: the argument over proof versus capture lets status show through interruption and sentence length rather than through tag inflation.
Lines 37-39: Sylvanas reads traffic, pinch points, roofs, and load-watchers before she grants the city any social meaning; this matches her sensory signature and exposition filter.
Lines 65-67: 'liked him at once' is still framed through utility—Corin earns approval for one useful sentence, not for charm—so Sylvanas's emotional register remains contained.
Lines 143-145: the split decision keeps Sylvanas tactical even in a relationship beat; trust is rendered as workflow, not confessed softness.

Lines 199-225: Jaina's explanation downshifts technical language for non-mages ('The farms grow it. The roads carry it.') while keeping magical consequence clear; this matches her audience-adjustment rule.
Lines 229-231: changing Jaina's synthesis beat to 'Same hands.' keeps her conclusion sharp, readable, and collaborative without slipping into planner abstraction.
Lines 275-289: the close expresses Sylvanas's reliance as operational fact ('half the answer went with her') instead of emotional translation, preserving her power posture and vulnerability rule.

fixes.rule: Jaina specificRules — clarity under breakthrough without negation scaffolding
fixes.before: "Not at the farms, not on the road. Here, after milling, during repack."
fixes.after: "After milling. During repack. The farms grow it. The roads carry it."

fixes.rule: perPOVCalibration — avoid abstract planner shorthand in Jaina's speech
Sylvanas specificRules — questions/orders and tactical diction should stay field-native
fixes.before: "If we pull a living operative out tonight, we may get the next tier before the district understands it's been opened."
fixes.after: "If we pull a live one out tonight, we may reach the hands above him before the district knows we've cut into it."

fixes.before: Because each had reached the same machine from a different door and hated leaving any piece of it untouched.
fixes.after: Because each had reached the same answer from a different door and hated leaving any piece of it untouched.

observations: Adverbs per page after revision: 1.12, well below the hard cap of 4 per page.
Passive-voice heuristic after revision: 3 matches across 321 sentences, or 0.93%, well under the 4% cap.
Lines 35-37, 175-183, and 243-259 all favor concrete observed behavior over explanatory labels, keeping showing ahead of telling.
No therapy-speak, modern scientific jargon, or planner terms from the style-guide avoid list survived in narration or dialogue after the fix pass.
The chapter uses filter words sparingly; where perception appears ('looked,' 'asked'), it serves actual action or speech rather than padded sensory distance.
Said/asked dominate attribution, with creative tags staying below the 8% ceiling and therefore compliant with the prose quality floor's plainness requirement.
Exposition integration: lines 35-37 and 199-225 keep worldbuilding tied to permits, wax, grain dust, ward flare, and residue; there is no detached narrator aside.
Power dynamics: lines 157-163 and 241-259 map control cleanly—Sylvanas drives with shorter tactical sentences, Jaina resists through more layered explanation, then snaps to one-line assent ('Fine').

Scene shape: the chapter opens with an environmental hook and active objective, pivots at the split (lines 143-167), pivots again at the assay reveal (lines 199-225), and resolves on cliff-edge forward pressure ('The capture could not.').
Speculative integration: magic is treated as labor, residue, pressure, shell, and failure mode rather than spectacle, which matches the high-domestication policy.
Subtext density: the Jaina/Sylvanas argument at lines 243-259 stays about proof and timing on the surface while actually testing trust, authority, and acceptable risk underneath.
Humor deployment is compliant: dry lines at 31, 105, 137, and 267 sharpen competence scenes, then thin out during the assay-room and capture-plan beats as horror and urgency rise.

fixes.rule: scene-level expositionPolicy — start at the answer, not a negation ladder
fixes.before: "Not at the farms, not on the road. Here, after milling, during repack."
fixes.after: "After milling. During repack. The farms grow it. The roads carry it."

fixes.rule: sceneShapeStandards + subtextDensityTargets — keep the conflict grounded in the actual disagreement
fixes.before: Because each had reached the same machine from a different door and hated leaving any piece of it untouched.
fixes.after: Because each had reached the same answer from a different door and hated leaving any piece of it untouched.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 37-39, 143-145, 227, and 275-289 stay lean, tactical, and rank-aware after removing briefing diction.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — short declaratives dominate, with occasional longer tactical chains at lines 37 and 241 that close bluntly.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — low image density, sourced from soldiery/pressure/body risk; no ornamental stacking.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — trust appears as operational reliance at lines 277-289, not as named softness.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — threat assessment -> capability judgment -> decision is intact in gate read, Havel scene, patrol, and capture planning.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — sightlines, exits, movement, dust, yard noise, and readiness dominate her page-level perception.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — dry needles at lines 23, 31, 105, 137, and 267 land as deadpan contempt or competence banter, not authorial wink.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — her dominant beats shorten into commands and clipped corrections, especially lines 157, 181, 241, and 263.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — magic is translated into utility and breach potential, never into awe.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — she notices route logic, discipline, cover, and who is useful; no explanatory military lecture intrudes.

ruleViolations: Line 183 originally drifted into intelligence-brief wording ('working assets, two layers'); fixed to a more field-native Sylvanas read.
Line 227 originally used 'embedded' and 'tiers'; fixed to 'inside' and 'handlers above them.'
Line 241 originally used 'living operative' / 'next tier'; fixed to 'live one' / 'hands above him.'

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — dialogue at lines 43, 85, 199-225, and 259 stays precise without turning modern-scientific.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — longer reasoning chains appear in the assay explanation, but breakthrough lines snap down cleanly.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — Jaina's lane here is mostly explanation, and she avoids ornamental comparison.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — strain shows through coldness, rolled sleeves, and tighter mouth lines rather than overt emotional naming.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — observation -> pattern -> mechanism -> consequence structure is clear throughout the assay-room scene.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — residue, dust, lamps, blue light, contamination traces, and bodily cold all fit her magical sensory fingerprint.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — the dry return at line 265 feels tired and honest rather than quippy.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — when challenged, she holds ground with calm precision and then compresses to 'Fine' once the joint plan is set.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — magic reads as craft, shell, trace, and trigger; never as spectacle.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — she explains only what the room needs and ties mechanism directly to immediate tactical consequence.

ruleViolations: Line 199 originally used a negation ladder ('Not at the farms, not on the road'); fixed so the breakthrough starts at the answer.
Line 229 originally summarized with 'One machine,' which read more like plan shorthand than Jaina's field explanation; fixed to 'Same hands.'
